author: bharath.vedartham
content: yes i don't have the context on the previous 2 apis being deprecated. I raised this PR cause i require them to get a p2p builder working e2e. I am happy to close my PR in favour of your PR which will contain the full context

author: jameshe5018
content: I agree for that api spec submission shouldn't be under pool it should be under validator

author: bharath.vedartham
content: you mean only the `submitSignedProposerPreferences` one right?

author: jameshe5018
content: yeah

author: bharath.vedartham
content: I followed the pattern similar to `submitPayloadAttestationMessages` https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/blob/master/apis/beacon/pool/payload_attestations.yaml#L50, so i thought `submitSignedProposerPreferences` would work similarly too

author: jameshe5018
content: the purpose of the api is more aligned with /eth/v1/validator/prepare_beacon_proposer and /eth/v1/validator/register_validator ( which is what it's trying to replace)

author: jameshe5018
content: it's a validator responsibility

author: jameshe5018
content: also wanted to ask , in terms of that event

author: jameshe5018
content: right now I'm interpreting it as 1 event per signed message, but I think it should be like an array right?  otherwise you're going to get a lot of events and it might clog the event stream

author: jameshe5018
content: aligning with the api that takes an array

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i assume the preferences channel to be pretty low volume anyways?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: I guess you would have to buffer those messages and periodically send the messages as an event right?

author: jameshe5018
content: yeah it will buffer and send

author: jameshe5018
content: that's fine to trigger it each time but also trying to get the intent of this

author: jameshe5018
content: how does the consumer want to use it

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
so a builder would want to listen to the preferences and cache them in a slot -> proposer preferences map. 
When the slot comes up, I would look up the cache and build the block according to the preferences

author: bharath.vedartham
content: I think i m not too opinianated on it being an array of messages or 1 message at a time as long as receive the preferences of the proposer for the given slot

author: jameshe5018
content: but do you want it based on each time the something submits preferences or just loop through the array sent and send each one as an individual event on the stream

author: bharath.vedartham
content: so how would the buffering work? would you send the event at the end of every slot then?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: as a builder, i think i would want the preference when it is received in the beacon node so i can keep my proposer preference cache up to date

author: jameshe5018
content: just based on consumer reading speed we just have an internal buffer

author: bharath.vedartham
content: so that by the time the proposal slot comes, i have the latest preference

author: jameshe5018
content: yeah

author: jameshe5018
content: ok i'll just do it as it for now

author: jameshe5018
content: 1 event for each signed message

author: jameshe5018
content: and we will need to test

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah i think attestations are much more high volume right in the same slot? and i think the event sends one attestation event for each attestation received?

author: jameshe5018
content: yes that's true

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah i assume 1 event for each message shouldn't be a problem since i think we will ideally have a burst of 32-64 messages at the start of epoch? I suspect it should be fine. But I ll keep an eye on this as I test

author: nflaig
content: one event per message is fine, that's how all existing events work and proposer preferences is on the lower end in terms of volume
